governanceNotice: Governance change: modifying this file changes the structured gate-policy authority. Ordinary implementation tasks must not change it without task/ADR/decision evidence.

categoryDefinitions.boundary: Guards an architecture or package boundary invariant. Architecture review may cite this category as boundary evidence.
categoryDefinitions.local-consistency: Guards local formatting, mirrored declarations, generated/help text, or repository-local coherence. This category is useful but is not boundary evidence.
categoryDefinitions.smoke: Runs an end-to-end smoke or packaging/build smoke.
categoryDefinitions.release-policy: Guards release, package, supply-chain, runtime readiness, or publication policy.
categoryDefinitions.meta-governance: Guards governance surfaces such as PR body format, aggregate check composition, or CI gate topology.

nonDeterminismDefinitions.aggregate: Composite or control-flow wrapper whose result inherits multiple leaf gates or execution branches rather than one reproducible repository-local judgment.
nonDeterminismDefinitions.external-state: Reads a live service, registry, remote artifact, or orchestration state that repository source alone cannot reproduce.
nonDeterminismDefinitions.host-environment: Depends on host OS, headed runtime, installed toolchain, downloaded platform artifact, or another machine-specific execution condition.
nonDeterminismDefinitions.local-authority: Reads an intentionally private, gitignored authority that is present only in the canonical local checkout.
nonDeterminismDefinitions.temporal: Uses wall-clock time or an expiry/sunset boundary as a judgment input.

tierDefinitions.pr-required: Runs in a GitHub branch-protection required context for pull requests.
tierDefinitions.pr-advisory: Runs for pull requests but is intentionally not yet a GitHub branch-protection required context; promotion to required is a separate governed change.
tierDefinitions.main-only: Runs only on main push outside pull-request required contexts.
tierDefinitions.local-only: Produces an applicable verdict only for a declared self-hosted layout. Public CI may execute the local runner but receives an explicit not-applicable result because no private layout is declared.
tierDefinitions.manual-only: Runs only by explicit local or workflow-dispatch invocation.

enforcementConstants.description: The pull-request integration shard ids and their count are owned by the rewrite-ci workflow matrix; consumers resolve that authority through this manifest declaration instead of embedding the sequence or count.

localStop.selectionPolicy: Measured below 1 second on 2026-07-14, executes a repository-local static check, and does not run tests, builds, packaging, or network requests.

nonDeterminism.reason: The local PR approximation composes test, build, and external-state leaves and has no independent reproducibility guarantee.

gates.tierReason: Local PR approximation script is not invoked by rewrite-ci; GitHub PRs use tiered jobs instead.
consumerScope: local PR approximation command

allowlistPolicy.notes: Aggregate composition is governed by this manifest and package.json until G3 derives/checks it.

evidence: Composite runner only; positive controls are declared by its leaf gates.

consumerScope: GitHub pull request body

allowlistPolicy.notes: No human-authored PR allowlist; PR body must satisfy the bilingual template contract. Mergify merge-queue verification PRs are narrowly skipped when authored by mergify[bot], using a mergify/merge-queue/* head ref, and carrying the Mergify queue payload marker.

consumerScope: GitHub pull request changed-file paths
GitHub pull request governance declaration body section
manifest-derived protected governance surfaces

allowlistPolicy.notes: Break-glass is declared in the PR body only for urgent main recovery and must record reason, scope, and a follow-up governance task. The checker verifies declaration shape and references, not semantic truth.

nonDeterminism.reason: This control-flow marker is eligible only when GitHub reports same-head-SHA source-validation proof, and its check names are routed outside the required-context namespace.

gates.command: echo "Reusing successful source validation for the same head SHA."
consumerScope: Mergify synthetic merge-queue pull_request edited events for mergify/merge-queue/* branches

allowlistPolicy.notes: This is not a human-authored PR bypass. Mergify synthetic queue metadata edits reuse same-SHA source proof under non-required check names; human edited events keep the required names and full validation.

evidence: Control-flow reuse marker; it performs no source validation and may run only with same-SHA proof.

consumerScope: TypeScript project references

allowlistPolicy.notes: TypeScript diagnostics are fail-closed.

evidence: No dedicated negative TypeScript compilation fixture is registered.

consumerScope: repository JavaScript and TypeScript lint surface
application triadic/projection consumer boundary: packages/application/src/local-controller-service.ts cannot import runtime ArtifactStore or readAuthoredDocument from kernel authored storage
GUI renderer kernel-store boundary: packages/gui/src/renderer/** cannot import kernel storage primitives
GUI renderer import boundary: renderer must consume window.harness bridge only
GUI IPC primitive placement: ipcMain.handle and ipcRenderer stay in their approved Electron files
kernel/application production-source physical I/O import boundary: fs/fs/promises/child_process imports are rejected outside explicit port implementation debt files

allowlistPolicy.notes: Kernel deep-import known debt and W3 port physical-I/O file exceptions are externalized from eslint.config.mjs; ESLint imports these modules directly and each entry carries decision/task plus reason evidence. The application projection and renderer kernel-store boundaries add no allowlist or exemption.

allowlistPolicy.notes: Adjacency violations run at error severity behind an exact no-growth ratchet. Every explicit deep subpath carries an owner, sunset condition, and machine-enforced production-consumer ceiling.

GUI related task closeout must run this local E2E entry and attach evidence; it is not part of GitHub CI or check:local.

nonDeterminism.reason: The applicable verdict depends on tasks.root from a private harness/harness.yaml layout, resolved directly or through Git worktree commondir; a checkout with no self-host declaration is explicitly not applicable.
